prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: What My Sister Buried
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Court-Ordered Lifetime
Tagline: Where every secret comes home.
Theme: Silence is just fear wearing cashmere.
Omen: a child drawing stairs
Relationship dynamic: unresolved hometown romance
Props: snow globe, blood-adjacent tea towel, unopened letter

Cast:
- Mara Bennett | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=lawyer | threat_aura=0.33 | space=toolshed described as donation overflow | secrets=has an unsent email that would destroy a family
- Cole Cross | archetype=divorced_redemption_dad | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=hardware store owner | threat_aura=0.25 | space=unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files | secrets=covered for someone out of shame; still has access to a property he does not own
- Tess Winterson | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=real estate agent | threat_aura=0.57 | space=old family office described as just storage | secrets=may die first or survive unbelievably; knows about a half sibling no one asked for
- Tess Hale | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=therapist | threat_aura=0.75 | space=retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence | secrets=inherited documents she should have burned
- Elias Lark | archetype=divorced_redemption_dad | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=marina manager | threat_aura=0.51 | space=old storm shelter from before the divorce | secrets=ignored a warning text on the worst possible night
- Jenna Sutton | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=boutique owner | threat_aura=0.43 | space=church basement described as seasonal materials | secrets=saw the wrong person near the lake once and never shut up about it; keeps screenshots like religious texts
- Cole Lark | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Wyatt Bell | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Cole Cross is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Mara Bennett says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Winter Hollow.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Cole?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Cole. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Mara. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The snow globe is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Tess."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
